title: Hartmannia tetraptera
taxonomy: Hartmannia tetraptera is classified under the family Cunoniaceae and order Oxalidales. It is a species of flowering plant native to New South Wales in Australia. The classification of Hartmannia tetraptera places it within the broader taxonomy of the Cunoniaceae family, which consists of about 30 species. The family Cunoniaceae is part of the larger angiosperm clade, encompassing flowering plants. This classification indicates Hartmannia tetraptera's unique position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hartmannia tetraptera is a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as a type of milkweed vine or Hartmannia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. Bioactive constituents of Hartmannia tetraptera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Hartmannia tetraptera is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
